sections.title: 一、阅读理解 第一节 A
sections.instruction: 阅读下面语言材料，从每题所给的 A、B、C、D 四个选项中选出最佳选项。
sections.passage:
**Questions:** Do you love stories? Who is your favourite character(人物)?

| Name | Content |
| --- | --- |
| **Li Wei** | I enjoy reading stories. Pi Pilu is my favourite character. He is not good at studying, but he is kind and always ready to help others. He has a lot of great experiences(经历). I dream of having a brother like him. |
| **Liu Fei** | I love stories about princesses(公主), like Snow White and Cinderella. I want to be a princess. Mum tells me that a princess must be friendly, hard-working and confident(自信的). I think Mum is right. |
| **Ma Dong** | Stories are not true. But they are fun to read. And they teach me lots of things, such as love and friendship. I will be a writer when I grow up. |

questions.prompt: Who is Pi Pilu?
options: C. A character in a story.
questions.explanation: 根据第一栏中 'Pi Pilu is my favourite character.' 可知，他是故事中的一个角色。

questions.prompt: Liu Fei's mum thinks that a princess must be _______.
questions.translation: 刘斐的妈妈认为公主必须是_______。
questions.explanation: 根据第二栏中 'Mum tells me that a princess must be friendly, hard-working and confident.' 可知，妈妈认为公主必须是勤奋的。

questions.prompt: What does Ma Dong think of stories?
questions.explanation: 根据第三栏中 'But they are fun to read.' 可知，他觉得故事很有趣（interesting）。

questions.prompt: What is Ma Dong's dream?
questions.explanation: 根据第三栏中 'I will be a writer when I grow up.' 可知，他的梦想是成为一名作家（写故事）。

sections.title: 一、阅读理解 第一节 B
sections.instruction: 阅读下面语言材料，从每题所给的 A、B、C、D 四个选项中选出最佳选项。
sections.passage:
One day, a small mouse looked out of his room. He saw a hamburger. The small mouse said, "I'll get it and have it for breakfast." But he remembered his mom's words. "Always wait before you get a hamburger." So he waited in his room. Then he heard a quiet "Miaow" and he knew a cat was there. He said to himself, "I'm happy I listen to my mom and learn to wait."
He waited for an hour, and then he heard a quiet "Woof, woof". "A dog!" The small mouse thought. If the dog is there, the cat won't be there. He ran out of his room but he met a cat. When the cat finished her breakfast, she said, "I'm happy I listen to my mom and learn a second language(语言)."

questions.prompt: What did the small mouse see when he looked out of his room?
questions.translation: 小老鼠朝房间外看时看到了什么？
questions.explanation: 根据第一段 'He saw a hamburger.' 可知，他看到了一个汉堡。

questions.prompt: Why did the small mouse think the cat was out of his room?
questions.translation: 为什么小老鼠觉得猫在房间外面？
questions.explanation: 根据第一段 'Then he heard a quiet "Miaow" and he knew a cat was there.' 可知，他是因为听到了猫的叫声。

questions.prompt: What did the cat have for his breakfast?
questions.explanation: 根据第二段最后 'He ran out of his room but he met a cat. When the cat finished her breakfast...' 可知，猫把小老鼠吃了作早餐。

questions.prompt: Which of the following is TRUE?
options: A. There was a cat and a dog in the room.
B. The small mouse didn't listen to his mom.
C. The dog caught the mouse.
D. The cat's mother was smart.
questions.explanation: 根据最后猫说 'I'm happy I listen to my mom and learn a second language' （模仿狗叫），暗示猫的妈妈很聪明，教了它第二语言。

sections.title: 一、阅读理解 第一节 C
sections.instruction: 阅读下面语言材料，从每题所给的 A、B、C、D 四个选项中选出最佳选项。
sections.passage:
Once upon a time, there lived a man by himself. He was blind, but he always took a lamp(灯) with him whenever he went out at night. One night when he came home after having dinner outside, he met a group of young men. They saw a blind man taking a lamp, so they made fun of him.
One of them asked, "Hey, man! You are blind and can't see anything! Why do you take the lamp with you then?"
The blind man said, "Yes, I am blind and I can't see anything, but the lamp is for the people like you who can see. They can see me coming and won't knock into me. That is why I take a lamp." The young men felt ashamed and said sorry to the man.
The story tells us that we should think before judging others. Always be polite to others and learn to see things from others' point of view.

questions.prompt: One night the man went out to _______.
questions.translation: 一天晚上那个人出去干什么了？
questions.explanation: 根据第一段 'One night when he came home after having dinner outside...' 可知，他是出去吃晚饭了。

questions.prompt: After the man told the young men why he took a lamp, the young men _______.
questions.translation: 在那人告诉年轻人他为什么拿灯之后，年轻人_______。
questions.explanation: 根据第三段 'The young men felt ashamed and said sorry to the man.' 可知，他们向他道歉了。

questions.prompt: From the story, we know _______.
options: B. we should help blind people
C. we should learn to see things from others' point of view
D. we should let the blind people walk first
questions.translation: 从这个故事中我们知道_______。
questions.explanation: 根据最后一句 'Always be polite to others and learn to see things from others' point of view.' 可知正确答案。

questions.prompt: Which is the best title for the passage?
options: D. A Blind Man with a Lamp
questions.translation: 这篇文章最好的标题是什么？
questions.explanation: 整篇文章主要围绕“拿着灯的盲人”展开描述，因此 D 最合适。

sections.title: 一、阅读理解 第一节 D
sections.instruction: 阅读下面语言材料，从每题所给的 A、B、C、D 四个选项中选出最佳选项。
sections.passage:
Once upon a time, there were two seeds(种子). They were good friends. They often talked with each other when they were free. One day, they had nothing to do. They lay on the ground. They looked up at the sky and had a chat with each other.
The first seed said, "I want to grow! Then I can enjoy the sun and the rain!" Later, it grew. It had a chance to enjoy the sun and the rain.
The second seed said, "I am afraid of growing up. If I grow, I have to live in the soil. Little animals may come up to me and eat me. How terrible! If I become a beautiful flower, a child will come and pick me. It is much better for me to wait. It's a dangerous way to be alive." It did nothing and refused to grow.
Spring came soon. A duck came up to the seeds. It ate the second seed and went away. See? We shouldn't act like the second seed. We should face our life bravely.

questions.prompt: What did they do one day?
options: B. They played by the river.
C. They flew in the sky.
D. They had a chat with each other.
questions.explanation: 根据第一段最后一句 'They looked up at the sky and had a chat with each other.' 可知，他们进行了聊天。

questions.prompt: The first seed wanted to grow because _______.
options: B. it wanted to be small
C. it could help each other
D. it could enjoy the sun and the rain
questions.translation: 第一颗种子想生长是因为_______。
questions.explanation: 根据第二段首句 'The first seed said, "I want to grow! Then I can enjoy the sun and the rain!"' 可知原因。

questions.prompt: In the third paragraph, the underlined word "soil" means _______ in Chinese.
questions.translation: 在第三段中，画线单词 “soil” 在中文里的意思是_______。
questions.explanation: 根据语境，种子生长就必须生活在泥土里，所以 soil 是指“土壤”。

questions.prompt: From the passage, we should _______.
options: C. run away when we are in trouble
questions.translation: 从这篇文章中我们应当_______。
questions.explanation: 文章最后一句明确写道：'We should face our life bravely.'，即我们应该勇敢面对生活。

sections.title: 一、阅读理解 第二节
sections.instruction: 阅读短文，从方框内所给的选项中选出可以填入空白处的最佳选项，其中有一个多余的选项。
sections.passage:
A man was walking in a forest. He was holding an axe(斧) in his hand. A little tree stopped him and said, "Please help me cut down those tall trees. Because of them, the sunlight cannot fall on me. [1]"
The man agreed and used his axe to cut down those tall trees. [2] But when the sun came out, the little tree lost water and became very thirsty. Later when a storm came, the little tree lost all its leaves and branches(树枝).
"You shouldn't ask the man to do that. Those tall trees can protect(保护) you. [3] You should cherish(珍惜) them. Now everything changes," a bird said.
[4] From then on, the little tree made good friends with the trees around him.

options: A. And I have no room to grow at all.
B. So you don't worry about heat, wind and rain.
C. Now you know how good it is without those tall trees.
D. The little tree felt happy and said thanks to the man.
E. After hearing the words of that bird, the little tree felt regretful(后悔的).
questions.explanation: 前句说到因为大树挡着阳光落不下来，A选项“并且我根本没有生长的空间”承接上文解释了为什么要砍树。

options: A. And I have no room to grow at all.
B. So you don't worry about heat, wind and rain.
C. Now you know how good it is without those tall trees.
D. The little tree felt happy and said thanks to the man.
E. After hearing the words of that bird, the little tree felt regretful(后悔的).
questions.explanation: 前面说那个人砍掉了大树，这里接D“小树觉得很开心并向那个人道谢”最合适。

options: A. And I have no room to grow at all.
B. So you don't worry about heat, wind and rain.
C. Now you know how good it is without those tall trees.
D. The little tree felt happy and said thanks to the man.
E. After hearing the words of that bird, the little tree felt regretful(后悔的).
questions.explanation: 鸟解释了大树的作用是保护小树，所以B“这样你就不必担心炎热、风和雨了”最符合语境。

options: A. And I have no room to grow at all.
B. So you don't worry about heat, wind and rain.
C. Now you know how good it is without those tall trees.
D. The little tree felt happy and said thanks to the man.
E. After hearing the words of that bird, the little tree felt regretful(后悔的).
questions.explanation: 在鸟解释后，小树的反应应当是后悔，随后跟周围树成为好朋友，因此选E。

sections.title: 二、完形填空
sections.instruction: 阅读短文，理解其大意，从 A、B、C、D 四个选项中选出可以填入空白处的最佳选项。
sections.passage:
The wolf and the fox wanted to eat the rabbit, but it wasn't [1] to catch him. One day the wolf said to the fox, "You go home and [2] in bed. I will tell the rabbit you are dead. When he comes to look at you, you can jump up and [3] him." "That's a good idea," said the fox.
He went [4] right away. The wolf went to the rabbit's house and knocked at the door. "[5] is it?" asked the rabbit. "It's the wolf. I've come to tell you that the fox is dead." Then the wolf went away.
The rabbit went to the fox's house. He looked in [6] the window. He found the fox lying in bed and his eyes were closed. He said to himself, "Is the fox [7] dead? If he's not dead, he'll catch me when I go near him." So he said, "The wolf says the fox is dead. But this doesn't [8] a dead fox. The mouth of a dead fox is always [9]." When the fox heard this, he thought, "I'll [10] him that I'm dead." So he opened his mouth. The rabbit knew that the fox wasn't dead, and he ran away as quickly as he could.

questions.explanation: 从下文要用计谋可知，直接抓兔子并不容易（easy）。
lie in bed 意为“躺在床上”。
狼给狐狸出主意，让狐狸趁机抓住（catch）兔子。
go home 意为“回家”。
敲门时询问是谁在外面敲门用 Who is it?。
through the window 表示“透过/穿过窗户”。
真的，真实地用 really。

questions.translation: 但这看起来不像一只死狐狸。
questions.explanation: look like 意为“看起来像”。

questions.translation: 死狐狸的嘴巴总是张开的。
questions.explanation: 根据后文狐狸听了之后张开嘴（opened his mouth）可知，兔子骗狐狸说死狐狸的嘴总是张开（open）的。

questions.translation: 我要向他证明我已经死了。
questions.explanation: show sb. that... 意为“向某人展示/证明……”。

sections.title: 三、语篇填空
sections.instruction: 阅读短文，在空白处填入一个最恰当的选项使短文通顺、连贯（本题已转换为单选题形式）。
sections.passage:
Peng Chao is a university student without arms. When he was taking the exam in Tongji University, he was offered a special desk. With his feet, the young man [31] the exam in the same time as other students. Later, he entered the university's law school.
In 1995, Peng was born in [32] village in Sichuan. He lived a happy and healthy life. But, at the age of six, Peng [33] touched a live electrical transformer. He lost both of his [34]. After returning from hospital, Peng's parents taught him how to live by [35] with his feet. Later, his father helped him learn how to use his feet [36]. Finally, the boy could write [37] his feet.
In 2015, Peng [38] the National College Entrance Examination (Gaokao) for the second time. He was given an extra 45 minutes. He got 603 [39]. He was admitted(准许……进入) to Sichuan University, Law School.
"It is a little inconvenient(不方便的) for me to be armless, [40] I still want to keep up with everyone else, and do what others can do," he said.

questions.explanation: 描述过去发生的事情，句子使用一般过去时，动词用 finished。
表示“在四川的一个村庄”，用不定冠词 a 表泛指。
这里修饰动词 touched，应当使用副词 carelessly（粗心地，不小心地）。
因为失去了两只手臂，名词用复数形式 arms。
by himself 是固定短语，表示“靠他自己，独自”。
learn how to do sth. 为固定表达，疑问词how后接动词不定式 to write。
write with one's feet 意为“用脚写字”，介词 with 表示工具或手段。
根据时间状语 In 2015 可知是过去发生的事，用一般过去时 took。
分数 603 是复数概念，后面的名词 point 用复数 points。
前半句表示“虽然没有双臂有些不便”，后半句表示“但我仍想跟上其他人”，前后有转折关系，用 but。

sections.title: 四、阅读与表达
sections.instruction: 阅读短文，根据短文内容选择最恰当的一项（本题已转换为单选题形式）。
sections.passage:
One spring day, a rose blossomed(绽放) in a forest. She greeted her neighbors, including a cactus(仙人掌), a pine and other plants. Then, she said to the cactus, "I'm the most beautiful flower here, but you are full of thorns and so ugly!"
Day after day, the rose never stopped being unkind to the cactus. The cactus, however, never got sad or angry. Instead, he always calmly replied, "Every life has a purpose in existence."
As summer came, the weather became hot and dry. The rose began to wither, but the cactus grew well. He had a wide root system and was good at storing water in his body.
One day, the rose saw a bird make a small hole in the cactus with its beak(喙). The thirsty bird drank the water inside the cactus through the hole.
"It must hurt," said the rose.
"Yes," the pine replied, "but the cactus doesn't want to see his bird friend suffer."
The rose was so thirsty that she asked the cactus for water. He kindly agreed. The bird filled its beak with water and watered the rose's roots.
The rose was ashamed(羞愧的) of what she had said to the cactus in the past. From then on, she stopped judging others by their looks.

questions.prompt: Why did the cactus manage to grow well during the hot and dry weather?
options: A. Because of its wide root system and ability to store water in its body.
B. Because it was protected by the rose and other trees.
C. Because it grew in a wet and cool area of the forest.
D. Because it did not need any water at all.
questions.translation: 为什么仙人掌在炎热干燥的天气里还能长得很好？
questions.explanation: 根据第三段中 'He had a wide root system and was good at storing water in his body.' 可知，它有宽广的根系和强大的储水能力。

questions.prompt: How did the thirsty bird drink water?
options: A. It drank the dew from the rose's leaves.
B. It made a small hole in the cactus with its beak.
C. It got water from the rain during the storm.
D. The pine tree collected water for it.
questions.translation: 口渴的小鸟是怎么喝到水的？
questions.explanation: 根据第四段中 'One day, the rose saw a bird make a small hole in the cactus with its beak. The thirsty bird drank...' 可知，小鸟是用喙在仙人掌上啄了一个小洞来喝水的。

questions.prompt: What lesson did the rose learn from her experience with the cactus?
options: A. She should always grow in hot weather.
B. She should be proud of her own beauty.
C. She shouldn't judge others by their looks.
D. She should not ask others for help.
questions.translation: 玫瑰从她与仙人掌的经历中得到了什么教训？
questions.explanation: 根据文章末句 'From then on, she stopped judging others by their looks.' 可知，她学到了不应当以貌取人。

questions.prompt: Which word best describes the character of the cactus based on the story?
questions.translation: 根据故事，哪个词最适合形容仙人掌的性格？
questions.explanation: 仙人掌不计较玫瑰先前的嘲讽，在玫瑰缺水时慷慨地为它和鸟儿分享自身的水分，表明了仙人掌的善良与慷慨大度（Kind and generous）。
